classes.name: mediapack.medium.Medium

methods.name: mediapack.medium.Medium.update_frequency
documentation:
Computes parameters' value for the given circular frequency

Notes
-----

Implemented in derived classes

methods.name: mediapack.medium.Medium._compute_missing
documentation:
Computes the required constant parameters missing from the definition

Notes
-----

Implemented in derived classes

methods.name: mediapack.medium.Medium.from_dict
documentation:
Reads medium definition from a hashmap of params.

Load all possible & recognised parameters and run `_compute_missing` to complete
the definition.

Parameters
----------
parameters : dict
    Linking parameter names and values.

Raises
------
LookupError
    If the parameter definition is incomplete (missing parameters listed in EXPECTED_PARAMS).

Base class for medium definition and manipulation

All derived classes have a few class parameters explained below.

Attributes
----------

EXPECTED_PARAMS, OPT_PARAMS : list of tuple, class attribute
    List of all expected parameters (resp. optional parameters) for this medium to be well defined.
    The format is `(param_name, param_type)` where `param_name` is a `str` and
    `param_type` a `type`.
MEDIUM_TYPE : str, class attribute
    State the type of medium , used to determine the real physics hidden in the medium
    (to distinguish a fluid from and equivalent fluid for instance)
MODEL : str, class attribute
    State which type of propagation model to use.
name : str
    Medium's name (default to Generic Medium)
omega : positive float or -1
    Last circular frequency for which the parameters have been updated

classes.name: mediapack.eqf.EqFluidJCA

methods.name: mediapack.eqf.EqFluidJCA._compute_missing
documentation:
Computes the required constant parameters missing from the definition

For a JCA equivalent fluid, the shear modulus `N` is computed.

methods.name: mediapack.eqf.EqFluidJCA.update_frequency
documentation:
Computes the JCA parameters (see Notes on the class).

Parameters
----------

omega :
    Circular frequency of interest

Represents an equivalent fluid medium with the Johnson-Champoux-Allard approach

The Johnson-Champoux-Allard model is described in references [^footnote-1], [^footnote-2] and [^footnote-3].

# Attributes

phi

: Porosity

sigma

: Flow resistivity

alpha

: Tortuosity

Lambda_prime

: Thermal characteristic length

Lambda

: Viscous characteristic length

# Notes

Populates the instance with the following attributes (and a few others left undocumented)

`rho_eq_til`

: Frequency dependent equivalent density

`alpha_til`

: Frequency dependent Johnson's tortuosity

`alpha_prime_til`

: Frequency dependent Champoux-Allard's tortuosity

`K_eq_til`

: Frequency dependent equivalent compressibility

`c_eq_til`

: Frequency dependent equivalent speed of sound

# References

[^footnote-1]:
    4. 12. Johnson *et al.*, "Theory of dynamic permeability and tortuosity in fluid-saturated porous media", Journal of fluid mechanics 176(1), 1987.

[^footnote-2]:
    25. Champoux & J.-F. Allard, "Dynamic tortuosity and bulk modulus in air-saturated porous media", Journale of Applied Physics, 1975, DOI: 10.1063/1.349482.

[^footnote-3]: J.-F. Allard & N. Atalla, "Propagation of Sound in porous media: modelling sound absorbing materials", Wiley, 2009, ISBN: 978-0-470-74661-5.

classes.name: mediapack.eqf.EqFluidJCAL

methods.name: mediapack.eqf.EqFluidJCAL._compute_missing
documentation:
Computes the required constant parameters missing from the definition

For a JCAL equivalent fluid, the shear modulus `N` is computed.

methods.name: mediapack.eqf.EqFluidJCAL.update_frequency
documentation:
Computes the JCAL parameters (see Notes on the class).

Parameters
----------

omega :
    Circular frequency of interest

Represents an equivalent fluid medium with the Johnson-Champoux-Allard-Lafarge approach

The Johnson-Champoux-Allard-Lafarge model is described in references [^footnote-1], [^footnote-2], [^footnote-3],
[^footnote-4].

# Attributes

phi

: Porosity

sigma

: Flow resistivity

alpha

: Tortuosity

Lambda_prime

: Thermal characteristic length

Lambda

: Viscous characteristic length

k0prime

: Static thermal permeability

# Notes

Populates the instance with the following attributes (and a few others left undocumented)

`rho_eq_til`

: Frequency dependent equivalent density

`alpha_til`

: Frequency dependent Johnson's tortuosity

`alpha_prime_til`

: Frequency dependent Champoux-Allard's tortuosity

`K_eq_til`

: Frequency dependent equivalent compressibility

`c_eq_til`

: Frequency dependent equivalent speed of sound

# References

[^footnote-1]:
    4. 12. Johnson *et al.*, "Theory of dynamic permeability and tortuosity in fluid-saturated porous media", Journal of fluid mechanics 176(1), 1987.

[^footnote-2]:
    25. Champoux & J.-F. Allard, "Dynamic tortuosity and bulk modulus in air-saturated porous media", Journale of Applied Physics, 1975, DOI: 10.1063/1.349482.

[^footnote-3]: J.-F. Allard & N. Atalla, "Propagation of Sound in porous media: modelling sound absorbing materials", Wiley, 2009, ISBN: 978-0-470-74661-5.

[^footnote-4]:
    4. Lafarge, P. Lemarinier, J.-F. Allard and V. Tarnow, Dynamic compressibility of air in porous structures at audible frequencies, J. Acoust. Soc. Am. 102(4), 1997, pp. 1995-2006

functions.name: mediapack.utils.from_json.from_json
documentation:
Reads medium definition from JSON file filename. Raises an IOError if the file
is not found, ValueError if medium type is not known and a LookupError if the
parameter definition is incomplete.

One may set the optional argument 'force' to a medium type to force loading this one.

documentation: thermal conductivity [W.m^-1.K^-1]

functions.name: mediapack.utils.from_yaml.from_yaml
documentation:
Reads medium definition from YAML file filename. Raises an IOError if the file
is not found, ValueError if medium type is not known and a LookupError if the
parameter definition is incomplete.

One may set the optional argument 'force' to a medium type to force loading this one.
